earlier "not available for Claude Code" claim is WRONG. Extraction is
T2 rule-tier: parse the prefix; caveat — success usually carries NO marker,
so exit-0 must be recorded as INFERRED (is_error=false, no prefix) vs
PARSED (explicit) — the classifier distinguishes the two provenances.
Feeds tool_result_exit_code backfill for the largest origin in the archive.

Toolchain semantics (operator ask): PACK-A grows a built-in toolchain
registry — command → tool identity → language/ecosystem (pytest→python,
cargo→rust, tsc→typescript, go test→go, ruff/mypy→python-qa...), so
analytics primitives like "failure rate by language", "test latency by
ecosystem", "which stack causes the most retry spirals" come free.
Registry = data file + classifier:<hash>, extensible per-archive via the
same residue loop (L2).

## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Each spec declares owner/authority, watch inputs, metric/definition refs, proposer, judge/routing
policy, artifact target/version, schedule/backoff, budget, candidate queue, and promotion rule.
Runtime state records last observation/proposal/judgment/artifact bump, starvation/failure/paused
state, retry/backoff, and receipts. Pilot L1 recall relevance by pointing to 37t.17's implementation
and evidence stream; do not build parallel read-access analytics. Pilot L2 classifier residue with a
different proposer/artifact type. More loops activate only after both run without per-loop scheduler
or state forks and pass budget/authority review.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

L1 and L2 register through one declaration and execute through one scheduler/state machine. Their
watch, proposal, judgment, bump, pause/failure/starvation, backoff, and budget receipts are visible.
Removing the shared scheduler/state path breaks both pilot tests. No other loop has an active daemon
schedule at closure; horizon specs remain declarative.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION HOMES assigned 2026-07-13: L7 compaction-regret -> polylogue-gjg.3 (pre-existing design, richer); L1 recall-relevance -> polylogue-37t.17; config loops (1jc/37t.10) register as instances. The registry declares; those beads implement.
REGISTRY ADDITIONS 2026-07-13: three more loop instances designed later the same session — L11 DECLARATION RECALL (37t.2 adoption note: retrospective PACK-D detectors find undeclared corrections/claims, diff vs declared markers, per-agent recall score feeds skill/preamble revisions); L12 CURRICULUM (xv1u: watch query-run telemetry -> measure recipe value -> propose curriculum diff -> operator gate -> skill version bump); L13 CAPTURE-COVERAGE ERROR (3uw: sessions-known-to-exist vs archived, per origin, budgeted alerts). All follow the 5-tuple; register, do not fork.

L11 AUTHORITY: declaration-recall measures missed declarations and may propose skill/preamble revisions. It does not make markers mandatory or authorize blocking hooks. Any enforcement waits for the 37t.2 experiment receipt plus explicit operator adoption as a revocable policy assertion.

title: Alphabet pack v1: action-semantics + failure-kind rule classifiers (PACK-A/B)
description: Critical path for the pattern program (grounding catalog: load-bearing for 6/16 analyses, pure T2, no judge dependency). PACK-A over actions view (tool+command+path): test-run, build, lint, format, typecheck, vcs:commit/push/rebase/merge, deps-install, db-op, service-op. PACK-B over exit_code + is_error + output-text rules: assertion-fail, compile-error, timeout, oom, permission-denied, network-error, env-breakage (ambiguous residue explicitly unclassified — never guess). Every classifier versioned + content-addressed (classifier:<hash>) so match counts pin classifier versions in query hashes. AC: >=95% live-archive test-run/build command coverage (measured, cited); misclassification sample judged; catalog patterns #1 and #15 compile+run on fixtures using only registered tokens.
design:
PACK-A classifies action semantics over typed action/tool/command/path evidence: test-run, build,
lint, format, typecheck, VCS operations, dependency installation, database operations, and service
operations. PACK-B classifies failure kinds from structural `is_error`, provider/parsed numeric exit
codes, typed background status, and bounded output rules: assertion failure, compile error, timeout,
OOM/killed, permission, network, and environment breakage. Ambiguous residue remains unclassified.
Each pack and toolchain mapping is a content-addressed classifier definition with authority and
evidence refs. Parsed code 0 and structural success-with-unknown-code remain distinct.

These packs are the actions-only vocabulary consumed by avna PatternDefinition. Pattern execution
pins classifier hashes and EventOrderSpec, exposes captures/measures/overlap policy, and must prove
SQL-vs-Python metamorphic parity before mixed-stream tokens are admitted. L2 in rxdo.11 owns the
classifier-residue improvement loop; pack implementation does not create a second loop.
acceptance_criteria:
1. PACK-A/B declarations are content-addressed, discoverable, and version all rule/toolchain inputs.
2. Live-corpus measurement shows >=95% coverage for declared test/build commands, with cited frame
   and a judged error sample; residue and ambiguous cases are reported rather than guessed.
3. Structural is_error, parsed numeric code, typed background status, and unknown-code success remain
   distinguishable in seeded fixtures, including killed/OOM and environment breakage.
4. Grounding-catalog patterns #1 and #15 compile and run using only registered actions-only tokens,
   with captures and measures.
5. SQL and Python executions agree under metamorphic rewrites and the same EventOrderSpec; removing a
   production classifier rule changes/fails the proof. Mixed-stream syntax remains deferred.
notes:
EMPIRICAL UPDATE (operator observation confirmed, earlier claim WRONG): Claude Code exit codes ARE extractable from session JSONL — foreground Bash failures carry 'Exit code N' as tool_result text prefix (live archive: 4067x code-1, 1092x code-2, 205x code-0, plus 128/144/8), background task completions carry 'completed (exit code N)' (3184x). PACK-B gains a T2 extraction rule for the largest origin; MUST distinguish PARSED exit-0 (explicit prefix) from INFERRED exit-0 (is_error=false, no marker) as separate provenances. Also SCOPE ADDITION (operator): built-in toolchain registry — command -> tool identity -> language/ecosystem (pytest->python, cargo->rust, tsc->typescript, ruff/mypy->python-qa) as a data file + classifier:<hash>, enabling analytics primitives (failure rate by language, retry spirals by stack); extensible per-archive via the classifier-residue loop (rxdo.11 L2).
EXIT-CODE GROUNDING REFINED (operator correction, verified): Claude Code supplies a TYPED is_error boolean on every tool_result (live archive: 60,266 false / 5,630 true) — success/failure is T1 STRUCTURAL, not inferred; my earlier parsed-vs-inferred caveat applies only to the NUMERIC code. Refined grounding: (a) is_error = T1 success/failure signal, always present; (b) numeric exit codes = T2 text parse where present — foreground failures carry 'Exit code N' prefixes, background completions carry 'completed (exit code 0)' (3,186x); (c) background task notifications ALSO carry typed-ish status tags: completed 8,612 / failed 695 / KILLED 3,794 (the killed class = OOM/freeze forensics gold, cf. tonight's incidents); (d) is_error=false without numeric marker = success-with-unknown-code — harmless: PACK-B failure kinds only need codes on failures, which carry them.

title: Pattern-analysis grounding catalog: 16 analyses, tokens, tiers, soundness verdict
description: Full catalog: .agent/scratch/pattern-analysis-grounding-2026-07-13.md. Sixteen analyses each grounded (pattern expression, tokens with tiers + grounding sources, yield grain, operators proven necessary). SOUNDNESS: 14/16 representable with alphabet packs A-E; 2 need phase-2 M6 join (honest v1 approximations); 3 correctly pattern+relational compositions. NEW requirements named: token unions; match spans as registered ObjectRef judgment targets; MEASURES aggregations; unified MIXED unit stream as default alphabet. Demo work must cite these groundings.
design:
# Pattern-analysis grounding catalog (2026-07-13)

Purpose: enumerate every pattern-language analysis named in tonight's design
threads and ground each precisely — pattern expression, tokens with authority
tiers, grounding sources, yield grain, and which operators/tokens it proves
necessary. This is the soundness proof-by-enumeration for the avna row-pattern
design + alphabet.

Tiers: T1=structural (parser-asserted columns) · T2=rule classifier
(content-addressed classifier:<hash>) · T3=judged annotation (candidate→judge
lifecycle) · D=derived interaction token (computed from structure,
classifier-named).

## Alphabet packs

- **PACK-A action semantics (T2)**: test-run, build, lint, format, typecheck,
  vcs:commit|push|rebase|merge, deps-install, db-op, service-op. Grounding:
  actions view (tool, command, path); versioned rule sets, classifier:<hash>.
- **PACK-B failure kinds (T2, T3 refinement)**: assertion-fail, compile-error,
  timeout, oom, permission-denied, network-error, env-breakage. Grounding:
  tool_result_exit_code + tool_result_is_error (T1) + output-text rules;
  ambiguous residue → T3.
- **PACK-C interaction tokens (D)**: gap(t) = inter-message gap > t with
  same-session continuation (honest name "gap", NOT "confusion" —
  away-from-keyboard is structurally undecidable; docs must say proxy);
  boundary:resume (T1 — column exists today); context-injection (T1 once
  37t delivery receipts land).
- **PACK-D message speech-acts (T3; some T2 bootstrap)**: question,
  instruction, correction, approval, completion-claim (T2 prior art exists:
  demo/receipts.py high-specificity phrase rules + seeded sampling),
  compliance-with-correction (T3).
- **PACK-E outcome proxies (T2, from the avna resolution note)**:
  final-action-failed, edits-never-committed, ends-mid-error. Session grain.

## The catalog

Each entry: pattern · tokens(tier) · yield · requires · verdict.

1. **Retry spiral** — `match(( a:edit -> a:test-run AND fail:assertion ){3,})`
   Tokens: edit(T1), test-run(A/T2), assertion-fail(B/T2). Yield: match spans
   per session (start/end action ids, iteration count as measure).
   Requires: quantifiers(M1), groups, captures(M3). VERDICT: representable
   with alphabet v1 (packs A+B) + M1/M3. NOTE the contrast class #16 — spiral
   vs healthy red-green differs ONLY by failure-kind mix and terminal state;
   without PACK-B the pathology detector is dishonest.

2. **Same-target thrash** — `match(e1=a:edit -> fail -> e2=a:edit[path=e1.path] ->
   fail -> e3=a:edit[path=e1.path])`. Tokens: edit(T1), fail(T1), path
   equality across steps. Requires: M6 cross-step variable binding
   (MATCH_RECOGNIZE DEFINE-class; beyond regular languages). VERDICT: NOT
   representable in v1; the join operator is proven necessary by this + #7.
   Phase-2 as designed.

3. **Churn→abandon** — `match(m:correction -> m:correction ->[within:10m] $)`
   over the MESSAGE alphabet, session must end (anchor) with outcome proxy
   abandoned(E/T2 or T3). Tokens: correction(D/T3), $ anchor, outcome proxy.
   Requires: message alphabet (M7), anchors (M5), absence-free. VERDICT:
   representable with M5+M7 + PACK-D correction. The [within] link already
   exists.

4. **67ac claims-without-verification** — `match(m:completion-claim ->[no:
   a:test-run|a:build|a:typecheck|a:lint] $)`. Tokens: completion-claim
   (D/T2 — receipts.py rules), verification = UNION of PACK-A members (an
   alphabet feature: token unions/classes, trivially regex-native). Requires:
   absence links (M2), anchors (M5), MIXED alphabet in one pattern (messages
   AND actions interleaved in one ordered stream — the session event stream
   is naturally mixed; design decision: the default alphabet is the unified
   ordered unit stream, tokens carry their unit kind). VERDICT: representable;
   proves M2+M5+mixed-stream. This replaces 67ac's bespoke sampling pipeline
   with one query — the flagship receipt.

5. **Fix-span extraction (AI-D3 feeder)** — `match(f=fail:any -> ... ->
   s=a:test-run AND ok)` with captures f..s exported as spans. Requires:
   captures/match-as-unit (M3), lazy gap semantics, overlap policy (M8 —
   first-fix vs all-fixes changes counts). VERDICT: representable; proves
   M3+M8. Spans feed the fix-embedding index.

6. **Workflow shape mining** — not a single pattern: frequent-sequence mining
   over the PACK-A token stream, THEN each mined shape becomes a match()
   query users can run/save (query:<hash>). Requires: the alphabet only;
   mining is an analysis recipe (rxdo.8), not a language feature. VERDICT:
   language is sufficient as target; mining lives in recipes. Upgrades
   workflow_shape_distribution from precomputed to queryable.

7. **Fabrication screen (continuous)** — claim of a performed action with no
   matching action row: `match(c=m:completion-claim[claims-action] ->[no:
   a:*[matches(c.claimed_target)]] $)` — requires extracting the CLAIMED
   TARGET from the message (T3 extraction; T2 for stereotyped claims) and a
   join between the claim's extracted target and action predicates (M6
   variant with extracted-field binding). VERDICT: v1 approximation WITHOUT
   join: claim followed by NO action of the claimed KIND at all (kind from
   claim classifier), catches the gross case (claimed test-run, zero
   test-runs after) — representable with M2 + PACK-D; exact-target matching
   needs M6+T3 extraction. Both stages valuable; stage honestly.

8. **Steerability** — `match(c=m:correction -> (m:assistant AND NOT
   compliance(c)){0,k} -> m:assistant AND compliance(c))` — per-model
   turns-to-incorporate. Tokens: correction(T3), compliance-with-THAT-
   correction (T3 pairwise judgment — agent-judged, K/L machinery). Requires:
   M1 bounded quantifier, M6-lite (compliance references the captured
   correction — but as a JUDGMENT input, not a structural join: the judge
   receives (correction, response) pairs). VERDICT: pattern extracts the
   candidate spans (representable with M1+M3); the compliance labeling is a
   judgment recipe over the extracted pairs. Two-stage: pattern → judge —
   and that composition (match spans as judgment work-queues) is itself a
   design requirement now named: MATCH SPANS MUST BE VALID JUDGMENT TARGETS
   (ObjectRef kind for match spans).

9. **Gap-after-output (confusion proxy)** — `match(m:assistant[tokens>T] ->
   d:gap(5m))`. Tokens: gap(C/D), token counts (T1). VERDICT: representable
   with PACK-C + mixed stream; the honest-naming rule does the epistemics.

10. **Resume-boundary cost** — `match(^ -> d:boundary:resume -> m:*{0,k})`
    measuring re-establishment tokens after compaction. Tokens:
    boundary:resume (T1 — EXISTS), token/cost measures (T1). VERDICT:
    representable TODAY structurally; measures need M3 capture-with-measures
    (MEASURES clause — add to M3 scope: aggregations over matched span).

11. **Unresolved-at-end** — cross-session future cone (avna resolution note).
    VERDICT: NOT a single-session pattern; v1 uses PACK-E proxies as session
    tokens; the goal-graph (v3) owns the real semantics. Pattern language
    scope boundary CORRECTLY drawn at session; cross-session composition
    happens in the relational layer (pipeline over lineage + AI-D1 clusters).

12. **Subagent fan-out efficiency** — sessions spawning N subagents, k fail.
    The lineage is a TREE; sequence patterns see only the spawn events
    (`a:subagent{3,}` works for counting spawns — T1 token exists) but
    child-session outcomes are cross-session joins. VERDICT: spawn-side
    representable; outcome joins are relational (pipeline: match | join
    children | ...). Tree-pattern language remains frontier, correctly
    out of scope.

13. **Verification-before-claim rate** — the anti-67ac positive discipline:
    `match(a:verification -> m:completion-claim)` vs #4 as a ratio (rigor
    mechanism B: numerator/denominator result-set refs). VERDICT:
    representable; the RATIO is rxdo.9.2's machinery, not the language's.

14. **Interrupted→resumed continuation quality** — session ends mid-error
    (PACK-E) AND lineage child exists AND child reaches success. Cross-
    session again: session-level tokens + relational join on lineage.
    VERDICT: two patterns + a pipeline join; representable as composition.

15. **Permission-denial loops** — `match((a:* AND fail:permission-denied)
    {2,})`. Tokens: PACK-B permission-denied. VERDICT: representable with
    M1 + PACK-B. (Tonight's classifier-denial events would have matched.)

16. **Healthy red-green (contrast class)** — `match((a:edit -> a:test-run AND
    fail:assertion){1,} -> a:test-run AND ok -> a:vcs:commit)` — same tokens
    as #1 plus terminal success+commit. VERDICT: representable; exists to
    keep #1 honest (pathology = spiral WITHOUT this terminal).

## Operator-requirement matrix (what the enumeration proves)

- M1 quantifiers: required by 1, 8, 15, 16.
- M2 absence links: required by 4, 7; nothing else substitutes.
- M3 captures + MEASURES: required by 1, 5, 8, 10 (MEASURES scope addition).
- M5 anchors: required by 3, 4, 10.
- M6 cross-step binding: required by 2, 7-exact; correctly phase-2 (nothing
  in the v1 demo set hard-blocks on it; 7 has an honest v1 approximation).
- M7 message alphabet + MIXED unified stream: required by 3, 4, 8, 9, 10 —
  the mixed ordered unit stream should be the DEFAULT alphabet (decision).
- M8 overlap policy: required by 5 (and any counted match).
- NEW (named by this exercise): token unions/classes (4); match spans as
  ObjectRef judgment targets (8); MEASURES aggregations (10); session-scope
  boundary with relational composition for cross-session (11, 12, 14).

## Alphabet-requirement summary

PACK-A and PACK-B are load-bearing for nearly everything (1, 4, 5, 13, 15,
16) — they are the v1 alphabet work and pure T2 rule-classifier packs:
cheap, deterministic, content-addressed, no judge dependency. PACK-C needs
one derived token (gap) + two existing columns. PACK-D has T2 prior art for
its hardest member (completion-claim) and T3 for the rest — it gates 3, 4,
7, 8 and arrives via the dve1 seed ontologies. PACK-E is three T2 rules.

## Soundness verdict

14 of 16 analyses are representable within the proposed design (session-
scoped row patterns + unified mixed unit stream + alphabet packs A–E);
2 (same-target thrash, exact-target fabrication) require the phase-2 M6
join and have honest v1 approximations; 3 (unresolved, fan-out outcomes,
interrupted→resumed) correctly live as pattern+relational compositions
rather than language features — the session-scope boundary held under
enumeration. No analysis required a mechanism outside the already-designed
M1–M8 + four named additions (unions, span-refs-as-judgment-targets,
MEASURES, mixed-default-alphabet). The DSL design is sound for the demo
program; the alphabet packs are the actual critical path.

## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

The catalog is the requirements traceability artifact for PatternDefinition, not an alternate language
spec. Each analysis row must bind tokens to authority tiers and classifier/schema refs, embed
EventOrderSpec (partition, lineage, order source, ties, evidence grade, horizon), state result/match
grain, captures/measures/overlap policy, identify pattern versus relational stages, and name the honest
v1 approximation when exact semantics require M6 or mixed streams. PACK-A/B actions-only analyses are
the executable v1 gate; PACK-C-E and mixed streams remain later until that parity proof lands.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 16 catalog rows contain expression, typed tokens, authority/grounding refs, EventOrderSpec,
   yield grain, captures/measures/overlap, required mechanism, and soundness verdict.
2. Every row maps to a named implementation Bead or explicitly documented relational composition;
   exact-target M6 and mixed-stream gaps use honest staged approximations.
3. Catalog analyses #1 and #15 execute through PACK-A/B fixtures with SQL/Python parity; #5 exposes a
   match span usable as a judgment/evidence ref.
4. Same-timestamp/tie and changed classifier-version fixtures alter grade/identity rather than silently
   preserving a match.
5. A generated/checkable support matrix fails if an analysis names an unregistered token/mechanism.

title: Order-explicit row patterns with typed match sets
description:
Design capture: .agent/scratch/dsl-pattern-matching-design-2026-07-13.md. VERIFIED current state: seq() = gap-tolerant ordered subsequence over action rows with per-link [next]/[within:t] constraints (QuerySequenceConstraint) — correct base semantics, but a strict fragment of SQL:2016 MATCH_RECOGNIZE. Missing, ranked: M1 quantifiers on steps/groups ((edit->fail){3,} retry spirals); M2 ABSENCE links (claim ->[no: verification] end = 67ac as one query); M3 captures/match-as-unit-grain (matched spans become rows usable in pipelines + result_sets — the rxdo-native requirement); M4 alternation/grouping/optionality; M5 anchors; M6 cross-step joins (same-file retry, MATCH_RECOGNIZE DEFINE-class, phase 2); M7 alphabet generalization (messages stream next); M8 explicit overlap policy (hash-relevant: match counts are population claims). IMPLEMENTATION: SQL prefilter (existing seq lowering) + Python NFA with predicate edges over candidate sessions bounded rows; quantifier-free patterns keep pure-SQL fast path; pattern AST enters rxdo.2 canonical query hash; LALR colon-terminal pitfall applies. Literal string regex: Python REGEXP as bounded post-filter only (hard candidate cap, typed error unbounded), low priority. Utility receipts: pathology patterns, 67ac, AI-D3 fix-span extraction, workflow shapes user-queryable, churn detection. Spike output: grammar sketch + NFA prototype over a fixture session + match-grain result_set design. Related: polylogue-fnm.13 (set algebra doc), rxdo.2 (canonicalization), rxdo.10 (demos AI-D3), 67ac.

## Authoritative corrective scope (2026-07-13)

Pattern semantics must name the event order and evidence grade; timestamp order alone cannot support
absence, sequence, or abandonment claims. EventOrderSpec is embedded in PatternDefinition rather
than becoming another durable registry.
design:
# DSL review + structural pattern matching design (2026-07-13)

Operator questions: (1) is seq(A->B->C) properly designed — rigid adjacency or
gaps? modeling + utility unclear; (2) review the DSL as-is vs known designs,
find suboptimal/missing; (3) could a regex-EQUIVALENT mechanism apply — a
sublanguage for specifying the STRUCTURE of a session (and literal string
regex, worth it?).

## 1. What we actually have (verified in expression.py + docs/search.md)

- Lark grammar: fielded predicates, booleans, near:"...", count/date ranges,
  `with <units>` projection, pipeline stages (sessions where ... | group by
  ... | count) over unit sources sessions/actions/messages/observed-events.
- `seq(step -> step -> ...)`: session-level predicate over ACTION rows.
  Steps are action-unit predicates (AND-conjunctions allowed per step).
  DEFAULT SEMANTICS: ordered SUBSEQUENCE — unrelated actions between steps
  are allowed. Per-link constraints exist: `->[next]` = strict adjacency in
  the action stream; `->[within:5m]` = time bound. Compiled to
  QuerySequenceConstraint(kind next|within); SQL lowerer applies each step
  predicate to its own ordered action row and enforces order between steps.
- So the operator's adjacency worry is already answered correctly: gaps by
  default, adjacency opt-in. Two real caveats:
  (a) `[next]` adjacency is ALPHABET-RELATIVE (next ACTION row, not next
      message/block) — underdocumented, and the right mental model is
      "regex over the action token stream";
  (b) seq is a session FILTER only — the matched span is thrown away. You
      can find sessions containing edit->fail->edit, but you cannot get the
      matching spans out as rows.

## 2. Comparison against known designs

- **SQL:2016 MATCH_RECOGNIZE** (Oracle, Flink, Snowflake): PARTITION BY /
  ORDER BY / PATTERN (regex over symbols: quantifiers, alternation,
  grouping) / DEFINE (symbol predicates, may reference other symbols via
  PREV/FIRST — i.e. cross-step joins) / MEASURES (extraction) / AFTER MATCH
  SKIP policy. seq() is a strict fragment: fixed-length concatenation with
  per-step predicates. MATCH_RECOGNIZE is the closest production-grade
  "regex for row streams" and the right semantic target.
- **Flink/Esper CEP**: next (strict) / followedBy (relaxed) / within (time)
  — seq's link constraints mirror this exactly — plus what seq lacks:
  notFollowedBy (ABSENCE), times/oneOrMore (quantifiers), groups.
- **spaCy Matcher**: per-token attribute predicates + OP quantifiers — the
  "predicate token + quantifier" shape, evidence this UX works for
  non-programmers.
- **LTL/temporal logic**: eventually/until/always — cleaner theory, worse
  ergonomics; regex-style wins for operators.
- **Tree patterns**: session lineage is a TREE (forks/subagents); sequence
  patterns don't cover "session that spawned ≥3 subagents which all
  failed". Frontier, not this design.

## 3. What's missing (ranked)

M1. **Quantifiers on steps/groups**: `(edit -> test-fail){3,}` — retry
spirals, the single most demanded pattern class (pathology detectors).
M2. **Absence constraints on links**: `A ->[no: action:verify] B` — "claim
followed by NO verification before session end" is exactly 67ac's
completion-claims analysis as one pattern. CEP notFollowedBy.
M3. **Captures / match-as-unit**: matches become a unit grain — rows
(session_id, span, per-step bindings) usable in pipelines (`matches where
pattern(...) | group by ...`), projectable via with-units, storable as
result_sets (rxdo grain). Without this, patterns can filter but not FEED
analyses — the rxdo-native requirement.
M4. **Alternation + grouping + optionality**: `(A|B) -> C?` — trivial once
the engine is an NFA.
M5. **Anchors**: `^`/`$` per stream — "session ENDS unresolved".
M6. **Cross-step joins (backreference-class)**: "edit file X ... fail ...
edit the SAME file" — per-step variable binding with equality guards
(MATCH_RECOGNIZE DEFINE-style). Phase 2; regexes proper cannot do this,
row-pattern engines can.
M7. **Alphabet generalization**: same engine over messages (role/
material_origin tokens) and, later, sessions-as-tokens in a workspace
timeline. Actions first (today's alphabet), messages second.
M8. **Overlap policy**: default AFTER MATCH SKIP PAST LAST, opt-in
overlapping. Must be explicit or match counts are ambiguous (rigor:
match counts are population claims — the policy is part of the metric
definition, so it belongs in the canonical AST → query hash).

## 4. Implementation shape (pragmatic)

Don't lower full patterns to SQL. Two-phase:
1. SQL prefilter: existing seq lowering (or its relaxation: "session
   contains at least one row matching each step predicate") narrows
   candidate sessions cheaply.
2. Python NFA (Thompson construction; edges are unit predicates already
   compiled by the expression layer) over each candidate session's ordered
   unit rows. Sessions are bounded; the unit rows already stream through
   the query layer. Deterministic, unit-testable, no SQL gymnastics.
Fast path stays: quantifier-free, absence-free patterns keep the pure-SQL
seq lowering.
Grammar: extend seq() or introduce match(); colon-terminal LALR pitfall
applies (new terminals above FIELD_CLAUSE.4). Canonicalization: pattern AST
enters the rxdo.2 query-hash canonical form (quantifiers/links/policy all
hash-relevant).

## 5. Literal string regex — honest verdict

SQLite ships no REGEXP; FTS5 can't. Feasible as a Python-registered REGEXP
function used ONLY as a bounded post-filter after FTS/predicate narrowing
(hard cap on candidate rows, typed error if unbounded). Useful for shapes
like error codes/UUIDs/paths; cheap to add; never a primary scan. Low
priority, worth having; folds into the same bounded-post-filter machinery
the NFA uses.

## 6. Utility receipts (why this earns its complexity)

- Pathology detectors as patterns: retry spiral `(edit -> fail){3,}`,
  thrash `(edit A -> edit B -> edit A)` (needs M6).
- 67ac completion-claims: `claim ->[no: verification] $` — the flagship
  measured-result demo becomes ONE QUERY.
- AI-D3 you-solved-this-before: `fail -> ... -> success` span extraction (M3
  captures feed the embedding index of fix spans).
- workflow_shape_distribution upgraded from precomputed shapes to
  user-queryable patterns.
- Churn detection: `correction -> correction ->[within:10m] abandon`.

## 7. Answer to the Seq modeling question, direct

Default gap-tolerance is the correct base semantics (rigid chains almost
never match real streams); adjacency and time bounds as LINK decorations is
also right. What was under-designed is not the link algebra — it's that (a)
the alphabet and adjacency-relativity are implicit, (b) there are no
quantifiers/absence/captures, so seq stops one step short of being the
regex-for-sessions it wants to be, and (c) matches are not values. The
design direction: seq() grows into row-pattern matching a la
MATCH_RECOGNIZE, with matches as a first-class unit grain.

## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

PatternDefinition embeds EventOrderSpec: partition key; lineage-composition policy; typed unit kinds;
ordering source; tie policy; evidence grade observed|checkpointed|replay-verified; horizon/as-of
evaluation receipt; overlap policy; and match policy. A match set retains captures, measures,
overlap/order receipts, and relation-manifest identity; it is not a result-set alias. Land PACK-A/B,
actions-only v1, captures/measures, and SQL-vs-Python metamorphic parity before mixed streams. No
standalone EventOrder registry until independent identity/lifecycle is demonstrated.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

PACK-A/B execute over actions-only data in SQL and Python with identical captures/measures and
overlap behavior. Equal timestamps under different tie/evidence policies remain ambiguous or yield
distinguishable grades; they never silently establish sequence. Mixed-stream syntax is rejected as
deferred. Removing EventOrderSpec from production lowering makes the parity fixture fail.
notes:
ALPHABET GAP (operator probe: what ARE 'edit'/'test-fail'?). Verified: the pattern alphabet today = SemanticBlockType (~10 structural, parser-asserted tokens: file_edit/shell/git/search/web/subagent/...) + raw string predicates (command:pytest) + structural outcome (output:failed from tool_result_is_error/exit_code). 'edit' is a primitive; 'test-fail' is NOT — no test-run/build/lint/typecheck/vcs-verb/deps-install categories exist, and no failure-KIND semantics (assertion-fail vs compile-error vs timeout vs OOM vs env-breakage vs network) — yet retry-spiral pathology NEEDS failure kinds (red-green iteration is healthy; retrying env-breakage is pathological). DESIGN: the alphabet is an ONTOLOGY AT ACTION GRAIN and reuses the tags/annotation ladder verbatim: tier-1 structural (parser-asserted, ground truth); tier-2 RULE-DERIVED command/outcome classifiers (pytest|cargo test|npm test -> test-run) — deterministic, versioned, CONTENT-ADDRESSED (classifier:<hash>, same names->hashes family as metric:/tag:/ranker:) so any pattern-match count pins the classifier version in its metric hash; tier-3 judged/embedded — ambiguous commands + failure kinds classified from output text, flowing as action-grain candidate annotations through the judge lifecycle, with derived-scalar membership enabling FUZZY TOKENS in patterns (~test-fail = membership above threshold; threshold + classifier hash both canonicalize into the query hash). Token inventory the alphabet needs: action semantics (test-run, build, lint, format, typecheck, vcs:commit/push/rebase/merge, deps-install, db-op, service-op, deploy), failure kinds (above), message-grain tokens for the message alphabet (question, correction, instruction, approval — the dve1 seed ontologies at message grain), derived interaction tokens (operator-stall from timestamp gaps, compaction/resume boundary — boundary='resume' already exists as a column, context-injection events). Third instance of the same design pattern: authority ladder + content-addressed definitions + judge lifecycle, now at unit grain.
RESOLUTION SEMANTICS (operator probe: what does 'unresolved' mean, how do we know?). Honest answer: today it is NOTHING — no primitive exists; my anchor example borrowed future vocabulary. Design (4th instance of the authority ladder): TIER-1 structural proxies, each an honest named classifier (classifier:<hash>): final-action-failed; edits-never-committed (file_edit/write with no subsequent vcs:commit in-session); ends-mid-error (last tool_result is_error with no later success). Cheap, incomplete, explicitly labeled PROXIES. TIER-2/3: the dve1 outcome ontology (solved/partial/abandoned/question-opened/question-closed) as judged session-grain annotations — 'resolved' is fundamentally SEMANTIC (was the goal met) and needs reading the conversation. THE DEEPER MODELING POINT: resolution is not a session-local property — sessions are EPISODES; the thing that gets resolved is a GOAL/QUESTION entity that spans sessions (opened in A, resolved in B days later, possibly different provider). The resolution graph = question-opened/closed annotation events + cross-session linking via D1 convergent-question embedding clusters + lineage descendants. So '$ unresolved' is sugar for: terminal session state with NO resolution event in the session's FUTURE CONE (lineage children + later same-cluster sessions) — and it is TIME-INDEXED: 'unresolved AS OF <archive_epoch>' (rigor: unresolved-counts must carry the as-of epoch; query_runs already record archive_epoch, composes for free). Staging: v1 structural proxies with classifier hashes; v2 outcome annotations via the dve1 bootstrap; v3 goal-entity graph (D1 clusters + lineage = the future cone).

title: Lint: detect numbered-migration slot collisions at verify time
description: Evidence 2026-07-13 merge train: PRs #2794 and #2800 independently claimed source-tier migration slot 008; the conductor caught it during rebase, renumbered to 009, preserved the capture_mode column through the copy-forward, and bumped SOURCE_SCHEMA_VERSION — but detection relied on conductor attention during conflict resolution. With parallel lanes routinely adding durable-tier migrations, slot collisions are now an expected event class. Add a cheap verify-step: duplicate NNN prefixes within storage/sqlite/migrations/{source,user}/ fail devtools verify --quick (and the same check as a lab policy so devtools lab policy schema-versioning covers it). AC: two files sharing a slot in either durable tier fail the gate with both paths named; renumbering guidance in the message.
design:
Extend the schema-versioning policy with a structured durable-migration contention key:
(tier, target schema version, numbered slot). Duplicate prefixes/targets in source or user migrations
fail `devtools verify --quick` and `devtools lab policy schema-versioning`, naming both owners/paths
and renumber/rebase guidance. Expose the key in machine-readable output so 2yax and ei94 can reserve
one writer per tier/window before Git conflict time. Derived-tier rebuild versions remain a separate
contention class, not numbered durable migrations.
acceptance_criteria:
Two durable migrations claiming the same tier/slot or target version fail with both paths and owner
refs; non-colliding source/user windows pass independently. JSON output supplies the contention key
consumed by frontier/conductor fixtures. A replay of the 008/009 incident is detected before merge.

title: Archive-intelligence demo catalog: AI-D1 through AI-D9
description:
AI-D1–AI-D9 are product proofs over the shared query/metric/pattern/judgment/experiment contracts, not nine
new subsystems. Every claim binds definition and evaluation refs, frame and measurement authority,
privacy/excision policy, and the evidence needed for its claim class. Flagships are AI-D1, AI-D3, and AI-D9;
first external activation is D3. Observational outputs use honest names, while causal language
requires ExperimentDefinition receipts.
design:
## Thread 1 — Demos beyond the current list (embedding-grade, rxdo-native)

Rigor template for every demo below: the demo IS a recipe (rxdo.8) whose
steps are query:<hash> refs; every number carries metric:<hash>; cohorts are
result_set objects; sampled quantities carry bootstrap CIs (mechanism H);
headline claims pre-registered (C); embedding-derived numbers pin the
embedding model+version in the metric hash (303r.7's model-effect key — an
embedding upgrade is a confound, not a refresh).

D1. **Convergent questions** ("you have asked this 14 times"). Cluster
embeddings of authored-user question messages across ALL providers; surface
recurring unresolved questions with their N historical answers and whether
the answers agree (agent judges score agreement — K/L machinery).
Population claim (count) + judgment layer; cohort = question-messages
(material_origin honest). The single most "this tool knows me" demo.

D2. **Semantic drift of self**: per-quarter centroids of authored messages;
trajectory through topic space; drift distances with CIs (sampled). "What
you stopped and started caring about, measured."

D3. **You solved this before**: given a fresh error text, embed → nearest
past tool_result failures (tool_result_is_error=true) that were FOLLOWED by
success (exit-code transition within session) → surface the fix diff.
Retrieval precision@k measured on a labeled holdout (D holdouts). Uses the
v16 keystone columns + embeddings + lineage composition.

D4. **Answer-quality arbitrage**: embedding-matched question pairs asked to
different models over time; blinded pairwise judging (N elicitation, agent
panel + operator spot-check); Bradley-Terry per-model quality ON YOUR OWN
question distribution — procurement decisions from your archive, not from
benchmarks.

D5. **Idea genealogy**: trace a named concept through time: first
appearance, mutation points (embedding neighborhoods over sliding windows),
which sessions developed it, when it crossed into code (thread 4 join).
Rendered as a timeline artifact; the trace is a saved analysis DAG.

D6. **Novelty watch**: standing query (rxdo.5) over embedding space — a new
session landing far from every historical cluster emits a novelty finding
candidate. "You did something categorically new this week" as a
push-notification-grade event.

D7. **Redundancy atlas**: semantic near-duplicate mass (embedding clusters)
× physical lineage duplication (#2467 machinery) → "your archive is X%
retellings" with exactness-honest accounting; doubles as a compression
roadmap.

D8. **Fleet convergence forensics**: tonight's 30-lane corpus — did
independent lanes converge on similar solutions? Embed lane outputs/diffs,
measure cross-lane similarity vs a between-unrelated-tasks baseline. First
demo OF the multi-agent archive ABOUT multi-agent work.

D9. **Re-explanation tax**: embedding-cluster instances of the operator
re-establishing the same context across sessions; price it (metric:
authored tokens in re-explanation clusters × cost model). The continuity
sales pitch as a measured number: "context loss cost you N tokens / $X last
month."

## Cross-cutting: what makes all four threads one design

Every thread lands on the same three moves: (1) name it with a content
address (query:/metric:/ranker:/tag:/schema versions); (2) grade authority
explicitly (asserted vs judged vs derived; population vs sampled); (3) make
agents produce CANDIDATES that flow through the one judge lifecycle.
Nothing here adds a second architecture — it's the rxdo graph + rigor
mechanisms + annotation machinery, extended to tags, files, and taxonomy.

## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

AUTHORITATIVE CLAIM CORRECTIONS. D3 returns prior observed recovery candidates; adjacency-only and
captured-span evidence never becomes "the fix" without target/state linkage or judgment. D9 reports
repeated-context mass/cost; avoidable loss, tax, or savings requires matched context-policy
experiments. D8 actual resume is descriptive; its improvement evaluation uses matched resume
treatments, not deliberately divergent baselines. Abandonment is unresolved_inactive(H) from the goal
graph with right censoring. Counts are frame-exact under named definitions, never unqualified
population claims. CIs name their uncertainty source. Demos consume existing typed objects and render
through Selection x Projection x Render; no demo-specific durable object or daemon loop.

DEMO NAMESPACE. This catalog uses AI-D1 through AI-D9. The proof-world portfolio under polylogue-212 uses PF-D*. In particular AI-D8 is fleet-convergence forensics and PF-D8 is actual session resume; unqualified D8 is invalid outside a parent-local historical quotation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every AI-D1–AI-D9 child declares claim class, consumer proof, definitions/evaluation world, frame,
   measurement authority, privacy/excision, reproducible fixture, and falsification condition.
2. AI-D3 and AI-D9 enforce the corrected naming gates; AI-D8 rejects confounded divergent controls.
3. D3 runs first on a cold external archive with cited candidates and measured precision@k.
4. Any causal headline resolves to stc assignment/exposure/outcome receipts; otherwise it is
   observational/capability-only.
5. Demo outputs reuse query/result/finding/judgment/report contracts and create no parallel stores.
6. Closing the catalog requires every child reconciled and the AI-D1/AI-D3/AI-D9 public-safe proof receipts.
notes:
ANALYTICS ATLAS (operator: 'what analytics could polylogue do that we do not yet know it could' — full text in .agent/scratch/archive-intelligence-design-2026-07-13.md addendum): A. PROCESS MINING — derive your actual debugging/build workflow as a discovered state machine with transition probabilities from action streams (alpha/heuristic miner on tool events; never applied to AI-pair-work logs); Markov entropy rate = workflow stereotypy per model. B. SURVIVAL ANALYSIS — Kaplan-Meier session-abandonment hazard vs duration/cost/error-count ('after the 3rd failed test run P(abandon) doubles'); time-to-resolution for recurring question clusters. C. INFORMATION THEORY — compression distance (NCD) as embedding-free similarity cross-check; conditional-entropy formulaic-ness score per model; MUTUAL INFORMATION between injected recall context and subsequent success = the honest self-test of the continuity product claim. D. GRAPH — session-x-file co-editing communities vs the import graph (mismatch = hidden coupling code structure hides); lineage-tree stats (fork survival, subagent fan-out efficiency). E. QUASI-EXPERIMENTAL — interrupted time series around tooling adoptions (beads, testmon, dolt-server: archive knows the dates); matched recall-vs-no-recall comparisons (observational, named confounds, or pre-registered via rxdo.9.10). F. CHANGEPOINT — PELT on cost/error/vocabulary ('something changed June 12' narratives; ref 9l5.17); fabrication screen = standing pattern query for claimed-actions-without-tool-events (67ac generalized to continuous monitor). G. INTERACTION SCIENCE — operator stall-time after agent output as confusion proxy; correction-incorporation turns = per-model STEERABILITY score (novel eval metric from real usage); question-answer coverage rate per model (agent-judged). H. ECONOMIC — marginal cost per merged PR/closed bead over time; model-tier efficiency frontier (cost x judged quality via D4/rankers) -> routing-policy advisor with receipts. All rigor-native by construction (metric hashes, cohorts, sampled-only CIs).
OPERATIONALIZATIONS for challenged atlas constructs (full text: closed-loops-design-2026-07-13.md Part C): ABANDONMENT = goal episode ending without terminal success AND no lineage descendant AND no same-question-cluster revisit within horizon H; recent sessions are RIGHT-CENSORED (exactly what Kaplan-Meier exists to handle — naive ended=abandoned counting is the bug); H is part of the metric hash; ladder v1 proxies -> v2 outcome annotations -> v3 goal-graph. RECALL-PACK HELP = four tiers: h1 direct usage of injected items (weakest, cheap), h2 re-explanation reduction (D9), h3 pre-registered A/B (only causal tier), h4 REDISCOVERY-MISS detection — agent re-derives content that WAS in the archive but NOT injected (embedding match) = measurable recall miss; converts part of unknown-unknowns into known-misses; residual limit named honestly (never-re-derived failures stay invisible). CORRECTION ABSORPTION = behavioral, never acknowledgment ('you're right' then repeating = noise): violation predicate per correction (subset compiles to checkable rules — 'use X not Y' is string-checkable, rule-tier; rest judged over pattern-extracted pairs); score = time-to-last-violation in-session; the metric that matters most = RECURRENCE across sessions (same correction needed again, embedding-matched) — local compliance without durable absorption is the real steerability failure.
ABANDONMENT/SUCCESS REDESIGN (operator: 'success' is semantic; abandonment cannot be annotated in realtime — but problems/questions OPENED and resolutions/answers CAN be): the primary grounding flips from retrospective outcome labels to PROSPECTIVE declared open/close events via the 37t.2 inline protocol — ::goal/::question/::problem markers open episodes (an optional/advisory session-start ::goal marker may feed this when present), ::resolved/::answer/::blocked markers close them, refs link closures to openings across sessions. SUCCESS = declared closure (agent-declared tier, calibration-audited); ABANDONMENT = derived, never annotated: open episode with no closure in the future cone within horizon H (right-censored until H elapses). Ladder becomes: v1 structural proxies (PACK-E) for history; v2 declared open/close events going forward (exact, cheap); v3 goal-graph linking (refs + D1 clusters) for cross-session. dve1's outcome ontology repositions as BACKFILL for the pre-protocol corpus + audit tier for declared closures, not the primary.
Cross-link 2026-07-13: polylogue-9l5 epic = this atlas's earlier design (tower layering worth adopting; 9l5.7 = metric:<hash> precursor). Per-child mapping recorded on the 9l5 epic note. One program, one implementation.

[LEGACY FIELDS PRESERVED BY CORRECTIVE FOLLOW-UP 2026-07-13]

ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION:
Design capture: .agent/scratch/archive-intelligence-design-2026-07-13.md Thread 1. Nine demos beyond the current set, each rigor-native (recipe with query:<hash> steps, metric:<hash> numbers, CIs only on sampled quantities, pre-registered headlines, embedding model+version pinned in metric hashes per 303r.7): D1 convergent-questions (asked-N-times), D2 semantic drift of self, D3 you-solved-this-before (v16 exit-code transitions + embeddings + holdout precision@k), D4 answer-quality arbitrage (blinded BT on own question distribution), D5 idea genealogy, D6 novelty watch (standing query), D7 redundancy atlas (x #2467), D8 fleet convergence forensics (the 30-lane corpus), D9 re-explanation tax (continuity priced). Sequence after rxdo substrate + rigor mechanisms; D1/D3/D9 flagship candidates. Related: 212.11 proof world, 67ac, fcyf.

D7. **Redundancy atlas**: semantic near-duplicate mass (embedding clusters)
× physical lineage duplication (#2467 machinery) → "your archive is X%
retellings" with exactness-honest accounting; doubles as a compression
roadmap.

D8. **Fleet convergence forensics**: tonight's 30-lane corpus — did
independent lanes converge on similar solutions? Embed lane outputs/diffs,
measure cross-lane similarity vs a between-unrelated-tasks baseline. First
demo OF the multi-agent archive ABOUT multi-agent work.

D9. **Re-explanation tax**: embedding-cluster instances of the operator
re-establishing the same context across sessions; price it (metric:
authored tokens in re-explanation clusters × cost model). The continuity
sales pitch as a measured number: "context loss cost you N tokens / $X last
month."

## Cross-cutting: what makes all four threads one design

Every thread lands on the same three moves: (1) name it with a content
address (query:/metric:/ranker:/tag:/schema versions); (2) grade authority
explicitly (asserted vs judged vs derived; population vs sampled); (3) make
agents produce CANDIDATES that flow through the one judge lifecycle.
Nothing here adds a second architecture — it's the rxdo graph + rigor
mechanisms + annotation machinery, extended to tags, files, and taxonomy.

CORRECTIVE READING 2026-07-13: the earlier detailed D1-D9 catalog remains valuable as ideation, but
any conflicting "fix," "tax," population-exact, or abandonment wording is superseded by the
authoritative corrective contract in the design field and the child Beads.

MARKER GATE: prospective goal markers are optional until polylogue-37t.2's declaration-recall experiment and a later explicit operator policy. Demos accept missing declarations as unknown/censored and never treat protocol non-use as abandonment.

title: File/repo evidence: observed tree trajectories and graded reproduction
description: Own the session-to-repository evidence program that superseded 7xv and 7xv.1. Session streams alone support an observed tree trajectory, not an exact historical working tree. Stronger claims require explicit checkpoints and replay verification. The program supplies file biographies, roads-not-taken evidence, contribution provenance, cost-per-subsystem, dead-reference filtering for recall, private evaluation episodes, fleet conflict prediction, and semantic work comparisons without turning Polylogue into a VCS.
design:
EVIDENCE MODEL. Materialize file/repository observations with session/action refs, repository identity, worktree/root evidence, path and rename evidence, operation kind, observed pre/post content hashes where actually captured, command/tool outcome, and a coverage/degradation receipt. Repository identity survives multiple worktrees and renames and never relies on cwd alone when stronger git evidence exists.

GRADE EVERY TRAJECTORY:
- observed: only tool/action-derived deltas; missing human edits, generators, shell side effects, concurrent agents, and external processes remain explicit coverage gaps;
- checkpointed: an interval is anchored by captured git tree/file hashes or equivalent pre/post state;
- replay-verified: a bounded reconstruction or applied patch matches a captured checkpoint and verifier receipt.

PRODUCT CLAIMS. G1 file biographies, G2 roads-not-taken, G4 cost joins, G5 dead-context filtering, and G7 conflict hints may consume observed evidence with visible coverage. G3 is contribution provenance, never line authorship; survival claims require at least checkpointed linkage. G6 private evaluation episodes and G8 exact work comparisons require checkpointed or replay-verified state plus declared task/outcome authority. Proposed-but-unapplied material remains distinct from applied changes.

REPRODUCTION HARNESS (absorbed from superseded 7xv.1). Build an ordered work trace, a reproduction plan, and a disposable worktree at the recorded base. Prefer applying the produced patch or checking out the target commit and rerunning verifier-class commands rather than replaying every historical command. Classify actions as pure_read | safe_verify | mutating_patch | networked | secret_sensitive | interactive | unknown. Only pure_read/safe_verify auto-run; mutating work is confined to disposable worktrees; networked/secret-sensitive/unknown remain plan-only without explicit authorization. Receipts cite both original evidence and reproduction outputs.

DURABILITY. File observations and derived trajectories are rebuildable index/read-model state; operator judgments and promoted evaluation definitions use their owning durable assertion/experiment contracts. No second VCS, generic replay executor, or unsupported authorship ledger is introduced.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A seeded session with tool edits plus an uncaptured external edit renders an observed trajectory with the gap visible and cannot claim an exact tree, exact authorship, or replay verification.
2. A checkpointed fixture binds pre/post git tree or file hashes; deleting a checkpoint downgrades every dependent claim.
3. A replay-verified fixture creates a disposable worktree, applies the recorded patch/target, runs declared verifiers, matches the checkpoint, and emits a two-way receipt.
4. Unsafe, networked, secret-sensitive, interactive, and unknown actions are not automatically replayed.
5. Contribution provenance distinguishes proposer, applier, generator, and observed committer where evidence permits; it never silently renders model authorship.
6. G5 excludes or visibly degrades recall items whose file refs no longer resolve against the selected repository generation.
7. The active design contains the session-repo mapping and safe reproduction value from superseded 7xv/7xv.1; no reader must reconstruct it from closed Beads.
notes:
[LEGACY FIELDS PRESERVED BY FINAL CORRECTIVE PASS 2026-07-13]

ORIGINAL TITLE:
File/repo modeling: replay-grade session->tree trajectories and the eight products

ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION:
Thread 4 of archive-intelligence-design-2026-07-13.md. Replay = the validation benchmark (operator framing); the products: G1 file biographies (every conversation that shaped a file + rejected alternatives), G2 roads-not-taken corpus, G3 line provenance -> per-model code SURVIVAL analysis, G4 cost-per-subsystem, G5 dead-context filtering for recall packs (inject only context whose file refs still resolve — biggest continuity quality lever), G6 personal SWE-bench (real (state,task,outcome) triples for model evals), G7 fleet conflict prediction (fcyf consumer), G8 semantic work-diffs. Substrate gap: file_refs relation (session/action x repo-identity x path x pre/post content hash) — the resolved evolution of polylogue-a7xr.17's unresolved code_refs strings. Repo identity must survive worktrees/renames (tonight's fanout = stress test). NON-GOAL: becoming a VCS. Spike output: file_refs schema sketch + replay-fidelity benchmark definition + G5 prototype scope.

ORIGINAL DESIGN:
## Thread 4 — Files/repos: replay is the benchmark; the gold is eight products

Replay-grade modeling = from session streams alone (Edit/Write/Bash tool
calls + results + git operations), reconstruct working-tree state
trajectories. The capability itself is a VALIDATION HARNESS (as the
operator said); what it enables:

G1. **File biographies**: every conversation that ever shaped a file, with
rejected alternatives — `read --file <path>` = git blame for the WHY. The
code-archaeology product.
G2. **Roads not taken**: proposed-but-unapplied/reverted diffs as a
queryable corpus of rejected approaches.
G3. **Line provenance**: which session/model/prompt authored the lines of
current HEAD; enables survival analysis ("whose code survives 90 days") —
a rigorous per-model quality measure NO benchmark can give.
G4. **Cost-per-subsystem**: join actions' file paths × cost model — "the
daemon cost $X across 214 sessions."
G5. **Dead-context filtering for recall**: inject past context only when
its file references still resolve against the CURRENT tree — recall packs
stop citing code that no longer exists. The single biggest continuity
quality lever.
G6. **Personal SWE-bench**: replay yields (initial state, task, outcome)
triples from real work → private eval suite of YOUR tasks for judging new
models (composes with D4/K-O judging).
G7. **Fleet conflict prediction**: live file-touch trajectories per lane →
predicted conflicts before they happen (fcyf fleet observatory consumer).
G8. **Semantic work-diff**: compare two lanes/agents by file-state
trajectory structure, not diff text.

Substrate gap, concretely: a file_refs relation (session/action ×
repo-identity × path × pre/post content hash where derivable) — the
resolved evolution of a7xr.17's unresolved strings. Repo identity needs
care (worktrees, renames, same-repo-many-checkouts — tonight's fanout is
the stress test). Explicit non-goal: polylogue does not become a VCS; it
models what sessions DID to trees, keyed to git commits where visible.

title: Tags redesign: namespaces with separate membership, affinity, and confidence axes
description:
Informal tags remain namespaced, plural, and non-hierarchical by default, but one scalar cannot
represent asserted membership, semantic affinity, and classification confidence. These are three
independent constructs with axis-specific queries and provenance. Affinity never grants membership;
confidence measures an assertion/judgment, not similarity.
design:
tagged(item, tag) is asserted boolean/qualified membership with author/evidence/status.
tag_affinity(item, prototype) is embedding/model-derived similarity tied to a versioned prototype,
model, and evaluation world. tag_confidence(assertion) is calibrated uncertainty of a classifier or
judgment tied to its actor/execution context and definition. Comparisons or conversions across axes
fail closed unless an operation declares a versioned conversion. Informal membership may remain
informal forever. A prototype is a resource/definition used to calculate affinity, not tag identity.
DSL predicates and renderers name the axis (`tagged:`, `tag-affinity:`, `tag-confidence:`); the
ambiguous scalar `tag:x>0.7` is rejected.
acceptance_criteria:
Seed and query: high affinity without membership; asserted membership with unknown affinity; and a
low-confidence classifier assertion without changing membership or affinity. Axis-mixing operations
fail with a named conversion requirement. Prototype/model changes alter affinity receipts, not tag
identity. Cross-surface outputs preserve axis and provenance. Verify through production DSL lowering,
storage/read paths, and renderer tests—not a test-only replica.
notes:
[LEGACY FIELDS PRESERVED BY CORRECTIVE PASS 2026-07-13]
ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION:
Thread 3 of archive-intelligence-design-2026-07-13.md (nayuki tags-not-hierarchies applied). DECISIONS PROPOSED: no tree schema (hierarchy-as-convention via namespaces like topic/x, prefix queries); scalar tags as a THREE-TIER AUTHORITY LADDER: asserted-binary (user.db authority), asserted-scalar (judged confidence, calibrated agent judges), derived-scalar (embedding similarity to tag prototype, index-tier disposable). Tag prototypes content-addressed (tag:<hash> over exemplar refs + embedding model key) with mutable name pointers — same names->hashes pattern as query:/metric:/ranker:. DSL scalar predicates (tag:python>0.7), min/max fuzzy algebra, mixed-authority composition fails closed. Spike output: confirm the ladder against current TAG assertion rows + migration story + DSL grammar cost (colon-terminal LALR pitfall).

ORIGINAL DESIGN:
## Thread 3 — Tags: namespaces yes, trees no, scalars via three authority tiers

Nayuki's argument (single-parent hierarchies force false choices; tags are
multi-membership; queries are intersections) is already polylogue's
direction — TAG assertions are flat multi-membership. Adopt from the essay:
tag QUERIES as first-class set algebra (already have it via DSL) and
key-value tags where needed. Decisions:

- **No tree schema.** Hierarchy-as-convention via namespaces
  (topic/search, lang/python) — prefix queries give grouping free, no new
  machinery, no single-parent lie.
- **Scalar tags — yes, as an authority ladder, and THIS is the embedding
  unification**: a tag is a named region in meaning space with three
  assignment tiers:
  (a) asserted-binary — operator/agent says so; user.db authority;
  (b) asserted-scalar — judged confidence from annotation batches
      (calibrated agent judges, L);
  (c) derived-scalar — similarity(item, tag prototype) computed from
      embeddings for EVERYTHING, disposable/rebuildable (index-tier
      semantics, honest about being derived).
  Tag prototypes are content-addressed (tag:<hash> over exemplar refs +
  embedding model key) with mutable name pointers — the exact
  names→hashes pattern of query:/metric:/ranker:. Corrections (b) feed
  prototype revisions; the ladder is self-improving.
- **DSL**: scalar predicates `tag:python>0.7`; fuzzy set algebra uses
  min/max semantics, mixed-authority composition fails closed with a
  suggestion (grain doctrine applied to authority).
- Embeddings ↔ hierarchy: embedding space subsumes soft hierarchy
  (clusters nest approximately); a formal tree adds nothing analytical —
  navigation UIs can render soft nesting on demand.

title: Seed annotation ontologies + autonomous per-archive ontology bootstrap
description: Ship versioned seed annotation schemas over the existing annotation/batch/judgment substrate and bootstrap archive-specific ontologies as governed candidates. Seed families are activity, prospective goal events, observed outcome evidence, knowledge artifacts, and reusability. `unresolved_inactive(H)` is derived by the goal graph; abandonment is never inferred as a timeless annotation. Informal tags/affinity may nominate candidates but cannot become formal ontology facts without a judged annotation batch.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Prospective open/close/block events and structural outcome evidence remain distinct; absence of closure does not create an abandonment annotation.
2. The same open goal can become unresolved_inactive(H) only through 7yk5 with a named horizon/frame/evaluation receipt and right-censoring.
3. A high-affinity informal tag produces at most a candidate; formal ontology queries remain empty until a judged schema/batch assertion exists.
4. A rejected candidate leaves informal tags intact and preserves the rejection/evidence trail.
5. Multi-view bootstrap fixtures preserve cross-view disagreement, residue, rare samples, epoch scope, and privacy/excision behavior.
6. Historical outcome backfill renders its proxy/judged authority and cannot overwrite prospective goal events.
notes:
[LEGACY FIELDS PRESERVED BY FINAL CORRECTIVE PASS 2026-07-13]

ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION:
Thread 2 of archive-intelligence-design-2026-07-13.md. SHIP packaged annotation schemas on the EXISTING polylogue/annotations machinery: activity, outcome (solved/partial/abandoned/question-opened/closed), knowledge-artifact (decision/lesson/preference/fact/commitment-made), reusability (snippet/recipe/demo-worthy). Affect/stance NOT default (privacy: opt-in only). BUILD the bootstrap loop for archive-specific topics: embed+cluster sample -> agent proposes labels grounded in exemplars -> operator judges via elicitation session (rxdo.9.14) -> schema v1 registered -> agent batch backfill as candidates -> standing query watches cluster drift, proposes v2. Productizes the data-cartography workflow. DEP: rxdo.4 labels, rxdo.9.14 elicitation.

## Authoritative corrective scope (2026-07-13)

Formal ontology membership is not the high end of an informal scalar tag ladder. Informal tags and
affinity may nominate ontology candidates, but only schema/version/batch-governed judgment can
create ontology facts.

ORIGINAL DESIGN:
## Thread 2 — Ontologies: ship seeds, derive the rest

The machinery already exists (annotation schemas + batches + judge
lifecycle + rxdo.4 labels). What to SHIP is seed schema definitions, and
what to BUILD is the bootstrap loop.

Seed ontologies (packaged annotation schemas, versioned, judge-gated):
- **activity**: debugging | design | implementation | research | writing |
  ideation | ops | procurement — session/segment grain.
- **outcome**: solved | partial | abandoned | superseded | question-opened |
  question-closed — the基basis for D1/D3 and success analytics.
- **knowledge-artifact**: decision | lesson | preference | fact-established |
  commitment-made — feeds existing AssertionKinds; "commitment-made" is the
  sleeper (promises you made and forgot).
- **reusability**: snippet-worthy | recipe-worthy | demo-worthy — the
  curation feeder.
- Affect/stance deliberately NOT in the default seed set (privacy posture:
  private-by-default archives may opt in; never ship as silently-on).

Domain topics are NOT built-in — they are derived per-archive by the
**ontology bootstrap loop** (the productization of tonight's
data-cartography prompt): (1) embed + cluster a sample; (2) an agent
proposes taxonomy labels grounded in exemplar sessions per cluster; (3) the
operator judges/renames via an elicitation session (N — this is a judgment
workflow, blinding optional); (4) schema v1 registered in
annotation_schemas; (5) agent batch backfills as candidate annotations;
(6) a standing query watches cluster drift and proposes v2 when the space
moves. Custom ontology as a DERIVED, versioned, judged object — the
scaffolding universal, the taxonomy personal.

## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Candidate generation records source tag membership, affinity, classifier definition, confidence,
and evidence refs separately. Promotion creates a new annotation-batch assertion under a versioned
ontology schema after judgment; it never mutates an informal tag into a formal fact. Autonomous work
stops at candidate generation until governed experiment receipts justify more authority.

ORIGINAL ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

A high-affinity informal tag produces at most a candidate. Without an explicit judged batch it does
not appear in formal ontology queries. Promotion preserves the source axes and schema/batch refs;
rejection leaves informal tagging intact.

ORIGINAL NOTES:
BOOTSTRAP LOOP EXPANSIONS (operator: 'expand this idea'): (1) MULTI-VIEW — cluster on content embeddings AND behavioral signatures (action patterns via avna row-patterns, temporal rhythms, cost shapes) — different views yield different ontologies (topic vs workflow vs collaboration-style); (2) CROSS-VIEW AGREEMENT as taxonomy validation — a category isolated independently by text AND behavior views is real, reducing LLM-label hallucination; (3) UTILITY-DRIVEN REFINEMENT — rxdo.3 query-run telemetry shows which labels are actually QUERIED; unused labels decay, heavily-queried low-precision labels get split proposals — ontology optimizes for query utility (closed loop unique to polylogue: we log our own query usage); (4) ACTIVE EXEMPLAR SOLICITATION — route max-entropy boundary sessions to judgment first (active learning, resorter economics); (5) definitions-as-hypotheses — each label carries prototype + criterion, backfill measures precision, bad labels pruned; (6) EPOCH-SCOPED labels — categories can have validity windows ('your 2025 obsession'); (7) MATURATION PATH — stable categories upgrade from labels to structured extraction schemas (annotation machinery already supports fields: procurement -> {vendor, decision, price}); (8) subdivision on demand — split a cluster only when query traffic + internal variance justify it, namespaces emerge.

title: Active elicitation sessions: resorter loop with blinding (CLI + agent batch)
description: Rigor mechanism N (gwern.net/resorter adaptation). Elicitation session = (target set, dimensions, judge, blinding policy, budget); engine picks next comparison for max information (closest latent estimates / fewest observations) so ~50 items rank in ~100 comparisons. Surfaces: operator CLI riding p5g's fzf pattern (two panes, pick better, tie/skip), and agent batch mode (MCP feeding pairs, collecting verdicts). Part I blinding (rxdo.9.6) applies AT elicitation: provenance masked until session ends. Sessions durable, citing emitted judgments. DEP: K + M.
design:
## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Active elicitation uses exploration quotas so uncertain, minority, disconnected, and low-coverage
regions cannot starve behind exploitation. Selection receipts bind candidate pool, quota/policy,
item order/blinding, rubric, ActorRef/ExecutionContextRef, and definition versions.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

A seeded minority/disconnected region receives its declared exploration budget despite a larger
high-score region. Disabling the quota changes the production selection and fails the test. Every
prompt batch can be reconstructed from its selection receipt without exposing hidden labels.

title: ranker:<hash> — content-addressed aggregation models over judgment sets
description: Rigor mechanism M. A ranking is a DERIVED object: ranker:<hash> (Bradley-Terry MLE via iterative scaling ~100 lines pure Python — no scipy; majority; Dawid-Skene-weighted; mean) applied to a judgment set yields a ranking result-set (items, latent scores, uncertainty intervals, judge provenance). Same content-address discipline as query:/metric: hashes; judgment rows are the truth, fitted models are derived. Shares canonicalization machinery with rxdo.9.1. DEP: K + rxdo.2 substrate.
design:
## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Aggregation consumes explicit verdicts and produces a partial order. Disconnected components,
cycles, ties, incomparability, and insufficient evidence remain visible. A total rank is emitted
only when the ranker definition declares and justifies a tie-break/completion policy; otherwise no
fabricated ordering. Bind judgment-set, rubric, actor/context calibration, and ranker component refs.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

Seed disconnected components, a tie, an incomparable pair, and a preference cycle. Default output is
a partial order exposing each condition. A requested total rank without a declared completion policy
fails; a declared policy yields a distinct ranker identity and receipt.
notes: EXTERNAL REFERENCE ASSESSED (operator pointer, 2026-07-13): github.com/max-niederman/fullrank — Bayesian pairwise ranking, examined in depth. WHAT IT IS: Thurstonian probit model (p(i>j)=Phi(si-sj)) with a Unified Skew-Normal posterior, EXACT posterior sampling via the Arellano-Valle/Azzalini convolutional representation (real math — the probit-likelihood/normal-prior -> SUN conjugacy is the Durante-2019-era result), entropy-driven active pair selection. Python CLI+library. VERDICT: do NOT adopt as dependency — early-stage (26 commits, no releases), NO LICENSE specified (hard blocker for a public MIT repo), single-judge pairwise-only (no n-wise/Plackett-Luce, no multi-judge calibration weighting, no dimensions), and its author flags the active-selection heuristic as unproven/failing for some priors. WHAT TO TAKE (three things): (1) OUTPUT SHAPE — posterior SAMPLING yields ranking STATISTICS (P(i is top-k), pairwise P(i>j) marginals, rank distributions) — adopt this as the ranker contract's output shape regardless of engine; richer and more honest than point-estimates+intervals. (2) A second registered engine 'thurstone-sun' alongside the default pure-Python BT-MLE iterative scaling — ranker:<hash> makes engine plurality cheap; implement from the published math if wanted (NOT by vendoring unlicensed code); accept scipy dep only if/when this engine is requested. (3) Its documented active-selection failure modes reinforce: elicitation selection policy (rxdo.9.14) must be PLUGGABLE and LOGGED (policy id in session provenance) so policy quality is itself measurable — another rxdo.11 loop instance. Independent validation note: a stranger converged on the same loop shape (compare-until-confident + active selection + uncertainty-aware output) — the resorter-class design is convergent, which is evidence it is right.

title: Judges as actors (human or agent) with measured calibration
description: Rigor mechanism L. judge_ref identifies WHO judged: operator, or agent as (model + prompt hash) — a judge is a program. Nothing in the lifecycle assumes human (operator: 'judgments solely human is pointlessly limiting'). Per-judge, per-dimension calibration = agreement with consensus/gold on overlap items (Dawid-Skene framing: agent judges are noisy raters weighted by MEASURED agreement). DEP: K.
design:
## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Judge identity is ActorRef plus separate ExecutionContextRef, never a model-name scalar. Calibration
is stratified by stable actor/model family and exact prompt/tools/runtime/config context, with gold
coverage, abstention, tie/incomparable behavior, and drift windows visible. No universal JudgeSpec
table; a routing policy becomes a definition only after independent reuse/lifecycle appears.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

The same actor under two execution contexts has separable calibration. Missing gold or context
produces unknown calibration, not inherited confidence. Reports preserve abstain/tie/incomparable
rates and refuse unsupported cross-context pooling.

title: Comparative judgment objects: pairwise + n-wise, per-dimension
description: Rigor mechanism K (docs/design/analysis-rigor.md Part II). judgment shape compare(items[2..n], dimension, verdict = choice or full ordering, judge_ref, blinded, elicitation_ref) stored as assertion rows. Pairwise base case; n-wise orderings decompose Plackett-Luce-style so one shape covers both. Dimensions explicit — one comparison per dimension, no mushed 'overall' unless declared. Extends rxdo.4's judgment machinery; DEP rxdo.4.
design:
## Authoritative corrective contract (2026-07-13)

Verdicts are prefer-left, prefer-right, tie, incomparable, abstain, and insufficient-evidence, with
per-dimension support. Store rubric/version, blinded item order, ActorRef, ExecutionContextRef,
definition/evaluation refs, evidence refs, and rationale visibility. Do not create JudgeSpec.
acceptance_criteria:
## Corrective acceptance criteria (2026-07-13)

Seed every verdict. Tie and incomparable remain semantically distinct; abstain and insufficient
evidence do not become weak preferences. Receipts prove blinded order and exact actor/context.

title: Evidence ancestry walker: circularity, epoch skew, expired refs
description: Rigor mechanism I. Report renderer walks evidence refs and flags: circular ancestry (claim ultimately cites its own detector's output — extends rxdo.4 laundering guard), epoch skew between cited result-sets, expired never-promoted ops refs. Read-side only. Prior art: x35k freshness markers. DEP: rxdo.4 + rxdo.2.
design:
Traverse the typed evidence graph from finding/claim/report to query/result, source anchors,
judgments, metric/ranker/classifier definitions, and evaluation worlds. Detect cycles (including a
detector citing its own output), epoch/generation skew, incompatible definition versions, expired
unpromoted ops refs, stale/missing/ambiguous/quarantined anchors, frame-coverage drift, and private/
excised evidence. Return a typed ancestry report with path witnesses; never flatten distinct failures
into one invalid flag. Read-side analysis does not copy evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Seed a clean DAG and one case for each circular, epoch-skewed, definition-incompatible, expired,
stale, missing/ambiguous/quarantined, frame-drifted, and private/excised condition. The walker returns
the exact offending path/ref and deterministic status. 3tl.16 and bby.15 consume this production
report: unsafe ancestry blocks an unqualified supported claim/export, while explicitly allowed
forensic/stale policy remains visible in the manifest.

title: Standing-query alert budget: cooldowns, magnitude floors, deviation-first ordering
description: Rigor mechanism E — the multiple-looks guard. rxdo.5 re-tests many watched queries per convergence; naive thresholds = false-discovery machine with zero sampling error. Cheap tier: per-watch cooldown + minimum magnitude on top of rxdo.5's baseline-then-notify. Phase-3 tier: expected-drift bands from a baseline epoch window + global alerts-per-day budget spent largest-standardized-deviation-first. DEP: rxdo.5.
design:
Alert budgets govern repeated operational decisions, not sampling inference. Each standing query declares
cooldown, magnitude floor, baseline/evaluation window, degradation/frame requirements, and per-owner/global
budget. Candidates are ordered by declared standardized deviation or decision value; suppressed alerts
retain receipts. Multiple looks do not manufacture p-values over exact censuses. Budget/schedule state is
an rxdo.11-style operational contract, not one daemon loop per watch.
acceptance_criteria:
A repeated unchanged deviation emits once then respects cooldown; sub-floor changes do not alert; budget
exhaustion suppresses lower-priority candidates with receipts; a larger valid deviation wins ordering.
Frame degradation cannot silently trigger/clear an alert. Restart preserves required operational state,
and no inferential significance appears for exact enumeration.

title: Holdout cohorts: persistence class + planner enforcement
description: Rigor mechanism D. persistence_class='holdout' on cohorts/result_sets: excluded from exploratory queries by default; the RefOperand planner layer (rxdo.6) warns-or-fails on exploratory references; confirmation runs self-declare and are marked in query_runs. Gives demo claims the 'held on untouched data' leg. DEP: rxdo.6 planner + rxdo.2 persistence classes.
design:
Holdout is a persistence/access policy on a CohortDefinition or promoted relation manifest, not a second
cohort type. It records frame, selection definition, creation epoch, intended confirmation use, authority,
privacy/excision, and contamination events. Exploratory planning excludes holdouts by default; explicit
confirmation access emits a receipt and cannot retroactively restore a contaminated holdout.
acceptance_criteria:
Exploratory queries cannot read a seeded holdout. A declared confirmation run can, with a visible access
receipt. Accidental/unauthorized access marks contamination and prevents an untouched-holdout claim.
Reset/excision preserve the declared durability semantics, and cohort/result relation identities remain
distinct while sharing RelationManifest.

title: Pre-registration with graph-provable ordering + registered badge
description: Rigor mechanism C. A REGISTERED analysis = finding-candidate with expected set and statistic null, citing a query hash; a later run fills actuals. Ordering provable from the graph: registration timestamp < query_run timestamp AND run archive_epoch > registration epoch (tested on data that arrived after the hypothesis). Claims render 'confirmed (pre-registered)' ONLY under that ordering, else 'exploratory' — a provable badge, not moral credit. Prior art: polylogue-e5b5. DEP: rxdo.4 + rxdo.3.
design:
Pre-registration is a versioned assertion/ExperimentDefinition state whose timestamp and archive epoch
precede assignment/exposure/query execution. Bind hypothesis/expected result, query+MetricDefinition,
frame, exclusions, stopping, and analysis plan. Later executions link rather than mutate it. Registered
status is graph-provable; missing/late fields render exploratory. Reuse stc for experiments and finding
assertions for non-experimental registered claims; do not create another registry object.
acceptance_criteria:
A pre-execution registration plus later evaluation earns registered status; reversing timestamps,
changing the metric/query after exposure, or using data already observed renders exploratory/new-version.
Graph traversal proves ordering and all fields. Post-hoc metrics remain separated. Removing the ordering
check makes the production fixture fail.

title: Ratios as derived objects: numerator_ref + denominator_ref
description: Rigor mechanism B. No bare percentages in finding.v1 consumption: a proportion cites two result-set refs (or result-set + cohort).
